name: Regional Finance
description_short: Regional Finance offers personal installment loans of $2,501–$25,000 to subprime borrowers at 24%–45%+ APR through 350+ branches in 19 states.
description_long:
Regional Finance was founded in 1987 in South Carolina, starting with just four branches before expanding into one of the larger regional consumer installment lenders in the southeastern and central United States. Today it operates as a subsidiary of Regional Management Corp. (NYSE: RM), a publicly traded company headquartered in Greer, SC. The company is licensed in 19 states — Alabama, Arizona, California, Georgia, Idaho, Illinois, Indiana, Louisiana, Missouri, Mississippi, North Carolina, New Mexico, Oklahoma, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Virginia, and Wisconsin — with an estimated 188 to 350+ branch locations. It holds no CDFI, HUD, or NFCC certifications, as it is a for-profit consumer lender, not a nonprofit or federally designated community development entity.

Regional Finance specializes in personal installment loans ranging from $2,501 to $25,000 (up to approximately $35,000 in select states), with repayment terms of 24 to 60 months. APRs typically run from 24.00% to 35.99% for standard loans, with rates averaging around 45% for smaller amounts under $2,500. Borrowers can choose between unsecured personal loans and auto-secured loans that use a vehicle title as collateral — the latter may help applicants with weaker credit access larger amounts or more favorable terms. The application process includes a soft credit pull for prequalification (no score impact), and funds can potentially be available the same day after signing. There are no application fees or disclosed setup fees, though state-specific origination fees, acquisition fees, and account maintenance fees may apply depending on location.

Regional Finance's primary differentiator is its stated willingness to lend to subprime borrowers — people with poor or damaged credit histories who are typically declined by traditional banks and credit unions. The branch-based model provides in-person service that some borrowers prefer. The company also offers a dedicated mobile app (iOS and Android) and an online account management portal, enabling digital payments, AutoPay setup, and loan balance tracking. As a subsidiary of a publicly traded company, Regional Management Corp. operates with a degree of financial transparency that smaller private lenders may lack.

Regional Finance fills a real gap for subprime borrowers with limited options, but it carries significant caveats. APRs in the 24%–45%+ range are substantially higher than credit union personal loans or prime-rate bank products. The company holds a BBB rating of B and is not BBB accredited; customer reviews on BBB average just 1.11 out of 5 stars across nearly 800 reviews, with complaints frequently citing billing disputes, collection practices, and fee transparency. This is not a lender for borrowers who qualify for conventional financing — but for those facing emergencies or rebuilding credit with no better options, Regional Finance provides a structured installment loan rather than a short-term payday product, which can be more manageable over a multi-year repayment term.\n\nAs a financial institution, this lender competes with both traditional banks and newer fintech personal loan lenders in the consumer lending space. Borrowers seeking personal loans for bad credit may find more flexible terms through online lenders, while those focused on simplifying payments may benefit from debt consolidation loans with fixed rates. For credit building, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to improvement. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility into credit health, and credit counseling through nonprofit agencies can help consumers create sustainable budgeting plans.
address: 780 Lynnhaven Pkwy Suite 270, Virginia Beach, VA 23452

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee — this is a loan product, not a service subscription.

tiers.name: Unsecured Personal Loan
features: Loan amounts from $2,501 to $25,000 (up to ~$35,000 in select states)
Soft credit pull prequalification — no score impact
Same-day funding available after loan signing
State-specific fees (origination, acquisition, maintenance) may apply

tiers.name: Auto-Secured Personal Loan
features: May allow larger amounts or better terms for weaker credit profiles
Soft credit pull prequalification — no score impact
Same-day funding available after loan signing

pros: Founded in 1987 — 35+ years operating as a licensed consumer installment lender
Accepts applicants with poor or bad credit; explicitly targets subprime borrowers
Soft credit pull for prequalification — no hard inquiry until you accept an offer
Same-day funding available after loan signing
Both unsecured and auto-secured (vehicle title) loan options for flexibility
Mobile app (iOS and Android) plus online portal for payments and AutoPay management
Publicly traded parent company (NYSE: RM) provides financial transparency
cons: APRs of 24%–45%+ are significantly higher than credit unions or prime-rate lenders
BBB rating of B with no accreditation; averages 1.11/5 stars from ~776 BBB customer reviews
State-specific fees (origination, acquisition, account maintenance) add to total loan cost beyond the stated APR
Only available in 19 states — not a national lender
No CDFI, NFCC, or HUD certifications — purely a for-profit high-interest consumer lender
best_for: Borrowers with poor or bad credit who don't qualify for bank or credit union personal loans
People needing $2,500–$25,000 for emergency expenses or debt consolidation with no prime-rate alternatives
Borrowers who prefer in-person branch service when navigating a loan application
Residents of one of 19 licensed states seeking a structured installment loan over payday or title loan products
diagnosis: Regional Finance is best suited for subprime borrowers in its 19 licensed states who need $2,500–$25,000 and have exhausted lower-rate options at banks or credit unions. The structured multi-year installment format is more manageable than payday loans, but APRs of 24%–45%+ and low customer satisfaction scores make this a lender of last resort — not a competitive choice for anyone who qualifies for conventional financing.
services: Auto-secured personal loans using vehicle title as collateral
Online prequalification with soft credit pull (no score impact)
In-person branch service at 350+ locations across 19 states
Mobile app for iOS and Android — loan balance, payments, AutoPay
One-time payment processing via app or online portal
